nodes.name: Defence Committee
nodes.detail: House of Commons select committee scrutinising the MoD. Currently examining the Defence Investment Plan.
nodes.source: committees.parliament.uk, Defence Committee membership (accessed 7 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Public Accounts Committee
nodes.detail: Value-for-money scrutiny across public spending; running an inquiry into the affordability of the Defence Investment Plan.
nodes.source: General knowledge — verify before external use

nodes.name: MoD Ministerial Team
nodes.detail: Defence ministers following the 11 June 2026 reshuffle triggered by John Healey's resignation over Defence Investment Plan funding. PPS positions: both defence PPSs (Pamela Nash, Rachel Hopkins) resigned on 11 June; no successor appointments published as of 7 July 2026 — the Defence Secretary's PPS slots appear unfilled, which is itself engagement-relevant intelligence.
nodes.source: GOV.UK, Ministry of Defence ministers (3 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Shadow Defence Team
nodes.detail: HM Official Opposition frontbench for defence.
nodes.source: members.parliament.uk, Official Opposition: Ministry of Defence (accessed 7 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Business & Trade Committee
nodes.detail: Scrutinises the Department for Business and Trade, including industrial strategy and the US and EU economic relationships.
nodes.source: committees.parliament.uk, Business and Trade Committee membership (accessed 7 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Sub-Cttee on Economic Security, Arms & Export Controls
nodes.detail: B&T sub-committee scrutinising economic security, arms exports and export controls — the formal seam between trade and defence scrutiny. Full membership not yet in this dataset.
nodes.source: members.parliament.uk, member career page (accessed 7 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Joint Cttee on the National Security Strategy
nodes.detail: Joint committee of both Houses scrutinising the National Security Strategy. Full membership not yet in this dataset.
nodes.source: members.parliament.uk, member career page (accessed 7 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: DBT Ministerial Team
nodes.detail: Department for Business and Trade ministers. Only the Secretary of State is mapped in this dataset.
nodes.source: Business and Trade Committee correspondence, 17 Mar 2026 (SofS signature)

nodes.name: APPG Armed Forces
nodes.detail: Promotes better understanding of HM Armed Forces in Parliament. Officers shown; industry-funded programme recorded in the register.
nodes.source: Register of All-Party Parliamentary Groups, 23 Feb 2026 edition

nodes.name: APPG Armed Forces Community
nodes.detail: Issues affecting serving personnel, veterans and families. Secretariat: The Royal British Legion. The registered Chair (Calvin Bailey) was appointed a defence minister in June 2026 and by convention relinquishes the role; the chair is treated as vacant pending the next register edition.
nodes.source: Register of All-Party Parliamentary Groups, 23 Feb 2026 edition

nodes.name: APPG AUKUS
nodes.detail: Awareness of the AUKUS pact and its UK economic and industrial opportunities. Secretariat: Council on Geostrategy.
nodes.source: Register of All-Party Parliamentary Groups, 23 Feb 2026 edition

nodes.name: APPG Defence & Security Sectors Supporting Local Communities
nodes.detail: Social value contribution of the defence and security sectors.
nodes.source: Register of All-Party Parliamentary Groups, 23 Feb 2026 edition

nodes.name: APPG Steel & Metals-Related Industries
nodes.detail: A fair deal for UK steel and metals industries. Secretariat: UK Steel (Make UK).
nodes.source: Register of All-Party Parliamentary Groups, 18 May 2026 edition

nodes.name: UK Delegation to the NATO Parliamentary Assembly
nodes.detail: 18-seat cross-party, cross-House delegation to NATO's interparliamentary assembly, appointed by written ministerial statement (5 Nov 2024). Delegates debate defence and security policy with counterparts from 32 NATO states; a strong marker of sustained personal defence interest, especially for backbenchers without committee seats.
nodes.source: parliament.uk, UK Delegation to the NATO Parliamentary Assembly (announced by WMS 5 Nov 2024; accessed 8 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Select Committee on the Armed Forces Bill
nodes.detail: Ad hoc select committee examining the Armed Forces Bill 2024–26 (Service Justice System, defence housing, Reserve Forces). Only members with other defence relevance are mapped; full membership not yet in this dataset.
nodes.source: Parliament committee data via TheyWorkForYou / parallelparliament (accessed 8 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Defence-Industrial Constituency Champions
nodes.detail: ANALYTICAL GROUPING, not a parliamentary body: MPs with a major defence-industrial site in or adjacent to their constituency who have spoken on defence in Parliament within the past 18 months. Each link is labelled with the site and each member carries an individual citation. Inclusion criterion is footprint plus demonstrated engagement, never footprint alone.
nodes.source: Analytical category; per-member citations on each node

nodes.name: Dan Jarvis
nodes.role: Secretary of State for Defence (appointed 11 Jun 2026)
nodes.detail: Former Parachute Regiment officer; previously Security Minister. Appointed after John Healey's resignation over Defence Investment Plan funding.
nodes.source: GOV.UK, Ministry of Defence ministers (3 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Luke Pollard
nodes.role: Minister of State, Defence Readiness & Industry
nodes.detail: Lead minister for defence industry engagement — the primary ministerial audience for industrial and sovereign manufacturing arguments. Sits wholly within the defence zone: the industrial dimension is his portfolio, not a separate structural tie.
nodes.source: GOV.UK, Ministry of Defence ministers (3 Jul 2026)
nodes.constituencySource: General knowledge — verify before external use

nodes.name: Calvin Bailey
nodes.detail: Former RAF officer. Was Chair of APPG Armed Forces Community and a Defence Committee member until his June 2026 ministerial appointment; both roles relinquished by convention on joining the government, so he is connected here through the ministerial team only.
nodes.source: GOV.UK, Ministry of Defence ministers (3 Jul 2026)

nodes.name: Navendu Mishra
nodes.role: Interest signal: raised KNDS UK in the DIP statement

nodes.name: Pamela Nash
nodes.role: Former PPS to the Defence Secretary (resigned 11 Jun 2026)
nodes.detail: Resigned as John Healey's PPS over Defence Investment Plan funding, citing 'delays and difficulties' damaging public trust. A defence-committed backbencher now free of PPS speaking restrictions, with a public position on the defining defence argument of this Parliament.
nodes.source: Press reporting of MoD PPS resignations, 11 Jun 2026 (ITV News; The News, Portsmouth)
nodes.constituencySource: General knowledge — verify before external use

nodes.name: Rachel Hopkins
nodes.detail: Resigned as a defence team PPS on the same day as Healey and Nash over DIP funding. Same analytical profile: demonstrated defence commitment, now unconstrained by PPS restrictions.

nodes.constituencySource: General knowledge — verify before external use

nodes.name: Adam Dance
nodes.role: NMH urgent question; secured Westminster Hall NMH debate
nodes.detail: Leonardo helicopters (3,000+ jobs) in-constituency. Among the most active constituency-defence campaigners in the House.
nodes.source: Hansard via TheyWorkForYou / reporting: NMH urgent question 12 Jan 2026; Westminster Hall NMH debate 11 Feb 2026

cohorts.name: Publicly engaged with the Defence Investment Plan (Jun–Jul 2026)

members.summary: Issued the Defence Committee's formal response to the DIP; opened committee scrutiny hearings
Tabled the urgent question on the DIP (10 Jun); opened the Opposition Day debate on Defence Spending and Readiness (23 Jun)
Opened the Opposition Day debate on Defence Spending and Readiness for the Conservative front bench
Publicly welcomed the DIP's hybrid Navy investment and six new warships as good for Portsmouth jobs and naval heritage
Called the DIP "too little too late", arguing key capabilities including drones will not arrive until 2030
Commented on the DIP from a Gosport naval-community and shipyard perspective
Published a statement welcoming the DIP, highlighting personnel pay, £9bn for military family housing and the defence export facility
Made representations on behalf of KNDS UK during the DIP statement; the Defence Secretary undertook to visit Stockport
Resigned as PPS to the Defence Secretary over DIP funding, citing "delays and difficulties" damaging public trust
Resigned as a defence team PPS over DIP funding on the same day as Healey and Nash
PAC launched an affordability inquiry into the Defence Investment Plan
Contributed to DIP-related Commons sessions from the Opposition defence bench

cohorts.name: Spoke in the Typhoon Sovereign Capability debate (12 Nov 2025)

members.summary: Secured and opened the Westminster Hall debate, arguing for a new Typhoon production order to sustain the Warton production line and sovereign combat air capability
Contributed on export impacts and the Filton aerospace cluster's role in the Typhoon supply chain
Contributed on behalf of the BAE Samlesbury workforce and Lancashire defence-industrial jobs

cohorts.name: Spoke in the SDR Funding debate (15 Apr 2026)

members.summary: Raised the New Medium Helicopter contract and Leonardo Yeovil's 3,000+ jobs, arguing for sustained defence-industrial commitment
Raised Thales Templecombe apprenticeships and skills funding for the Royal Navy sonar programme
